target.name: Liquid AI
target.founder: Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, Daniela Rus

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Liquid AI is a Series A artificial intelligence software company founded by Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, and Daniela Rus, currently valued at $2.35B post-money on $287M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 same-sector peers at comparable or adjacent stages, spanning 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private firms including OpenAI, xAI, Twelve Labs, and Field AI.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $2.4B last post-money valuation, implying $4.7B. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company comp set yields a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range of $250M to $300B reflecting wide dispersion in the frontier-AI sector. The peer band reflects material heterogeneity: Safety-focused entrants (Safe Superintelligence at $32B, Thinking Machines Lab at $12B) command substantial premiums while earlier-stage generalist platforms (Physical Intelligence, World Labs both ~$5-5.6B) anchor the distribution lower.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $3.0B to $5.4B, with a median implied value of $4.1B. This suggests Liquid AI's current $2.35B valuation sits in the lower quartile relative to peer benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the durability of recent frontier-AI valuations, the target's differentiated IP position relative to well-capitalized competitors, and ongoing capital efficiency given sector-wide burn rates.
